segments.text:
No specific respiratory structures/organs
gas exchange by simple diffusion through surface:
Porifera
Cnidaria
Platyhelminthes
Rotifera
gas exchange by diffusion; transport of O 2 /CO 2
Annelida
Nematoda

Tracheal system – insects, some other terrestrial A rthropoda
Spiracle = opening
Trachea branch – every cell can do gas exchange with environment

Gill – gas exchange with water
filaments, lamellae increase surface area

No more net
movement of O 2

Fish use one-way water flow over gills
set up blood flow in opposite direction = countercurrent flow

lower pressure – suction
pulls water into mouth
push/pull water out
through gills

Ram ventilation
yellowfin tuna
an “obligate” ram ventilator
nurse shark (sleeping )
clearly NOT a ram ventilator
Terrestrial vertebrate lungs – pouches off pharynx

A respiratory pigment
reversible O 2 binding

uses Fe + as cofactor
uses Cu 2+ as cofactor
Chordates, annelids
Oxygen binding by respiratory pigments is cooperative
tissues with lower O 2 – pigment releases more O 2
